mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing activity, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 220 while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, real yields turn deeply negative, triggering portfolio rebalancing away from equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of observations meeting the inflation and rate thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly payroll gains exceeding 100,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of triggered months over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0% indicates severely constrained aggregate demand, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDP growth is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above 105 index level signals a mature manufacturing expansion phase, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >50% of months when INDPRO exceeds 105 during mid-cycle phases.
